title: MCP servers
subtitle: Connect external tools via the Model Context Protocol

empty.title: No MCP server configured
empty.subtitle: Add a remote HTTP/SSE server or configure a local process

bridge.title: Bridge configuration
bridge.intro: Enter these values in the Electron bridge on your PC. The token identifies your account — the backend will forward commands only to the bridge connected with this token.
bridge.jwtNote: Each user has their own token. The bridge using this token will only receive commands related to your account. The token expires every 7 days — come back here to copy a fresh one.
bridge.macGatekeeperBody: The bridge is ad-hoc signed but not notarized by Apple, so macOS blocks the first launch. Unblock it once with right-click on the app → Open, or run in Terminal (adjust the path if not in Applications):

banner.connectedDesc: The Electron bridge is running and serving the remote tools.
banner.disconnectedDesc: Start the Electron bridge on your PC to use MCP servers with Remote transport.

modal.name: Name *
modal.loadOnFirstTitle: If disabled, the tools of this server are not loaded into the chat: they stay usable only by an agent that includes them (hierarchical delegation).
modal.transportHttp: POST JSON-RPC requests to a remote HTTP endpoint.
modal.transportSse: Server-Sent Events connection to a remote endpoint.
modal.transportLocal: stdio process started by the backend (same machine). No bridge needed.
modal.transportRemote: stdio process on your PC, proxied via the Electron bridge (different machines).
modal.commandHintRemote: — executed on your PC via the bridge
